qas.q: What is the blood borne pathogen regulation number?
To whom does the blood borne pathogen regulation apply to?
qas.a: Anyone who is exposed to blood at work

qas.q: What two main diseases does this regulation attempt to prevent the spreading of?
Who was the instructor of our OSHA class?
How many different types of hepatitis are there?
Why are all five types of hepatitis considered hepatitis?
qas.a: Because they all have similar clinical presentation

qas.q: Is Hepatits caused by a virus or a bacteria?
What are some signs and symptoms of all 5 types of Hepatitis?
qas.a: Nausea, fever, fatigue, abdominal pain, diarrhea, dark-colored urine, jaundice, loss of appetite

qas.q: What types of Hepatitis are not carried in blood? How are they transmitted? Are they covered by the blood borne pathogen standard?
qas.a: Hep A and Hep E; through fecal contamination; no

qas.q: New cases of Hep A dropped __% since the vaccine became available in ____
How do you prevent Hep A?
The most common blood borne disease...
The Hep B vaccine is available in three...
How to prevent Hep B...
At risk populations of Hep B include...
qas.a: homeless, injection drug users, corrections (prison tattooing especially), hemodialysis patients, people who have HIV

qas.q: These two types of hepatitis do not have a vaccine.
Hepatitis C is most prevalent in these types of groups...
qas.a: Injection drug users, recipients of blood transfusions before 1992, people with HIV, hemodialysis patients

qas.q: Hep C more frequently occurs in people born between ____ and ____
Intermittent symptoms occur with this type of Hepatitis
How do you prevent against Hepatitis C?
A Hepatitis C drug regimen lasts usually for __ weeks.
A standard Hepatitis C treatment may cost to $_____
An aberrant form of HBV
If you've been vaccinated for HBV, then you're also protected from ___
This type of Hepatitis is transmitted through fecal contamination, but usually through contaminated drinking water.
Hepatitis E is prevalent in...
qas.a: India, China, Africa, Middle East, Central America

qas.q: Which types of hepatitis are carried in blood? Which types are carried through the fecal-oral route?
qas.a: B,C,D in blood; A and E in fecal-oral route

qas.q: _____ ________________ _____ is the virus that causes Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome.
What percentage of HIV-positive people don't know that have it?
CEHS stands for...
qas.a: Center for Environmental Health and Safety
